kings_guild.name: The King's Guild
kings_guild.description: Master artisans of the King's Guild each ply a different trade in a different quarter of the city. Work out who's who.
clues: Oldwall goes with at least two of Matilda, Weaver, and 8 coins.
At least one of Matilda, the Weaver, and the artisan with 16 coins belongs to the Hill Ward.
Blacksmith goes with exactly one of Edmund, Matilda, or Eastcheap.
Either Kingsford goes with Wulfric or someone in the Hill Ward goes with Wulfric.
Between Kingsford and 6 coins, one goes with Cedric and the other with Wulfric.
Cedric, Edmund, and Wulfric go with Carpenter, Weaver, and 6 coins, in some order.
Rowan's levy is lower than both the Carpenter and the artisan in Highrow.
More members of the Gate Ward are in the Clothiers' Guild than members of the Hill Ward.
Tanner goes with exactly two of Godfrey, Highrow, and 10 coins.
The Carpenter's levy is exactly 4 coins more than the artisan in Eastcheap.
Either Matilda belongs to the Joiners' Guild or both members of the Hill Ward belong to the Joiners' Guild (but not both).
Exactly two of these are true: Godfrey goes with 8 coins; Matilda goes with Blacksmith; Millpond goes with 14 coins.

fishing.name: The Fishing Derby
fishing.description: Anglers at the lake-country derby each landed a different fish from a different pond on a different lure. Work out who caught what, and where.
clues: Willowmere goes with at least two of Esther, Trout, and 4 lb.
At least one of Esther, the angler who landed the Trout, and the catch with 8 lb belongs to the West Watershed.
Bass goes with exactly one of Cody, Esther, or Cedarmere.
Either Pinehollow goes with Wade or someone in the West Watershed goes with Wade.
Between Pinehollow and 3 lb, one goes with Boone and the other with Wade.
Boone, Cody, and Wade go with Bluegill, Trout, and 3 lb, in some order.
Royce's weight is lower than both the angler who landed the Bluegill and the angler at Mossbank.
More members of the East Watershed are in the Sunfish Family than members of the West Watershed.
Perch goes with exactly two of Dale, Mossbank, and 5 lb.
The angler who landed the Bluegill's weight is exactly 2 lb more than the angler at Cedarmere.
Either Esther belongs to the Pike Family or both members of the West Watershed belong to the Pike Family (but not both).
Exactly two of these are true: Dale goes with 4 lb; Esther goes with Bass; Stillwater goes with 7 lb.
